rationale: The citing paper analyzes TV ad content effects on online search and sales and emphasizes bias from omitting channels, while the cited paper is about attribution vs. profit-maximizing multimedia budget allocation with carryover/interaction effects—so there is plausible conceptual overlap on cross-channel advertising measurement. However, the available match evidence is only a single DOI reference entry with no in-text citation context or explicit note that the cited model/method is used. Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop, but importance is not yet clear.
